dev.jeka.core.api.marshalling.xml

Class JkDomElement

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.marshalling.xml.JkDomElement

  • public final class JkDomElement
extends java.lang.Object
    Borrowed from VincerDom https://github.com/djeang/vincer-dom Wrapper for Element offering a Parent-Chaining fluent interface.

    The underlying element may exist or not. If the underlying element does not exist, a proxy element is used in place but write methods are disabled. The concrete w3c element can be created afterward using the make() method.

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      JkDomElement __ 

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      JkDomElement add(java.lang.String name)
      Adds a child element of the specified name on the underlying element.
      JkDomElement addSibling(java.lang.String name)
      Adds a sibling element of the specified name just before this one.
      JkDomElement apply(java.util.function.Consumer<JkDomElement> consumer)
      Runs the specified consumer with this element as argument.
      JkDomElement applyIf(boolean condition, java.util.function.Consumer<JkDomElement> consumer)
      Runs the specified consumer with this element as argument.
      java.lang.String attr(java.lang.String name)
      Returns the value of the specified attribute on this element.
      JkDomElement attr(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String value)
      Adds the specified attribute name/value on the underlying element.
      JkDomElement child(java.lang.String name)
      Returns the child first child of this element having the specified name.
      JkDomElement child(java.lang.String name, java.util.function.Predicate<JkDomElement> predicate)
      Returns the child first child of this element having the specified name and verifying the specified predicate.
      java.util.List<JkDomElement> children(java.lang.String name)
      Returns an unmodifiable list of the child elements having the specified name.
      java.util.List<JkDomElement> children(java.lang.String name, java.util.function.Predicate<JkDomElement> predicate)
      Returns an unmodifiable list of the child elements having the specified name and verifying the specified predicate.
      boolean exist()
      Returns true if the underlying element exist.
      JkDomElement get(java.lang.String name)
      Returns the first child element of the underlying element having the specified name.
      JkDomDocument getDoc() 
      org.w3c.dom.Element getW3cElement()
      Returns the underlying w3cElement.
      JkDomElement make()
      Creates the underlying element and its non-existing parents.
      static JkDomElement of(org.w3c.dom.Element element)
      Creates a VElement wrapping the specified element.
      static <P> JkDomElement of(JkDomElement parent, org.w3c.dom.Element element)
      Creates a VElement wrapping the specified parent and element.
      JkDomElement remove()
      Removes the underlying element from its parent children.
      JkDomElement removeAttr(java.lang.String name)
      Removes the specified attribute of the specified name from the underlying element.
      java.lang.String text()
      Returns the text pof the underlying element.
      JkDomElement text(java.lang.String text)
      Sets the specified text on the underlying element.
      java.lang.String toString() 
      java.util.List<JkDomElement> xPath(java.lang.String xPathExpression)
      Returns an unmodifiable list of elements matching the specified xPath expression.
      java.util.List<JkDomElement> xPath(javax.xml.xpath.XPathExpression xPathExpression)
      Returns an unmodifiable list of elements matching the specified xPath expression.

    • Field Detail

      • __

        public final JkDomElement __

    • Method Detail

      • of

        public static JkDomElement of(org.w3c.dom.Element element)
        Creates a VElement wrapping the specified element.

      • of

        public static <P> JkDomElement of(JkDomElement parent,
                                  org.w3c.dom.Element element)
        Creates a VElement wrapping the specified parent and element.

      • getW3cElement

        public org.w3c.dom.Element getW3cElement()
        Returns the underlying w3cElement. This element can be null if this VElement does not exist.

      • attr

        public JkDomElement attr(java.lang.String name,
                         java.lang.String value)
        Adds the specified attribute name/value on the underlying element.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the underlying element does not exist.

      • attr

        public java.lang.String attr(java.lang.String name)
        Returns the value of the specified attribute on this element. Returns null if no such attribute exists.

      • removeAttr

        public JkDomElement removeAttr(java.lang.String name)
        Removes the specified attribute of the specified name from the underlying element.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the underlying element does not exist.

      • text

        public JkDomElement text(java.lang.String text)
        Sets the specified text on the underlying element.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the underlying element does not exist.

      • text

        public java.lang.String text()
        Returns the text pof the underlying element. null if the underlying element does not exist.

      • add

        public JkDomElement add(java.lang.String name)
        Adds a child element of the specified name on the underlying element. This method returns the newly created element.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the underlying element does not exist.

      • get

        public JkDomElement get(java.lang.String name)
        Returns the first child element of the underlying element having the specified name.

        If no such element exist, this method returns a proxy element that let creation possible afterward.

      • children

        public java.util.List<JkDomElement> children(java.lang.String name,
                                             java.util.function.Predicate<JkDomElement> predicate)
        Returns an unmodifiable list of the child elements having the specified name and verifying the specified predicate. Returns an empty list if the underlying element does not exist.

      • children

        public java.util.List<JkDomElement> children(java.lang.String name)
        Returns an unmodifiable list of the child elements having the specified name. Returns an empty list if the underlying element does not exist.

      • child

        public JkDomElement child(java.lang.String name,
                          java.util.function.Predicate<JkDomElement> predicate)
        Returns the child first child of this element having the specified name and verifying the specified predicate. Returns null if the underlying element does not exist or no such named child exists.

      • child

        public JkDomElement child(java.lang.String name)
        Returns the child first child of this element having the specified name. Returns null if the underlying element does not exist or no such named child exists.

      • xPath

        public java.util.List<JkDomElement> xPath(javax.xml.xpath.XPathExpression xPathExpression)
        Returns an unmodifiable list of elements matching the specified xPath expression.

      • xPath

        public java.util.List<JkDomElement> xPath(java.lang.String xPathExpression)
        Returns an unmodifiable list of elements matching the specified xPath expression.

      • addSibling

        public JkDomElement addSibling(java.lang.String name)
        Adds a sibling element of the specified name just before this one. This method returns the newly created element.

      • remove

        public JkDomElement remove()
        Removes the underlying element from its parent children.

      • apply

        public JkDomElement apply(java.util.function.Consumer<JkDomElement> consumer)
        Runs the specified consumer with this element as argument.

      • applyIf

        public JkDomElement applyIf(boolean condition,
                            java.util.function.Consumer<JkDomElement> consumer)
        Runs the specified consumer with this element as argument.

      • getDoc

        public JkDomDocument getDoc()

      • exist

        public boolean exist()
        Returns true if the underlying element exist.

      • make

        public JkDomElement make()
        Creates the underlying element and its non-existing parents. Does nothing if the underlying element already exists.

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
